name: Annette Lee
description:
'''Annette Rochelle Lee'''{{efn|{{Lang-zh|c=李安|p=Lǐ Ān}}<ref name="钟-2026">{{Cite web |last=钟 |first=雁龄 |last2=蔡 |first2=欣盈 |date=22 May 2026 |title=李安处女作《梦想小店》拍接吻床戏 老公不吃醋只求“别亏钱” |url=https://www.zaobao.com.sg/entertainment/story20260522-9089569 |access-date=2026-05-27 |website=www.zaobao.com.sg |language=zh-Hans |archive-date=27 May 2026 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260527222136/https://www.zaobao.com.sg/entertainment/story20260522-9089569 |url-status=live }}</ref>}} (born 10 July 1992) is a Singaporean singer-songwriter, scriptwriter, actor and filmmaker. She is known for works such as "The Caifan Song" and ''Dream Stall''.

Lee joined [[SGAG]] in 2015, under the persona "Sue-Ann", before quitting to create her own shows and comedic sketches in 2020. She plays various personae, such as Chantelle, whom she describes as an "entitled" [[influencer|social media influencer]], and Susan, described as a "thrifty" [[Aunt#Cultural variations|auntie]] by Ruth Yeo of ''[[Channel NewsAsia]]''.

In 2021, Lee released "The Caifan Song", a parody of ordering [[cai png]], as part of ''The Ann & Ben Show'' with [[Benjamin Kheng]]. She described the song as her "proudest achievement" in 2023, and was her [[breakout role]], according to Chong Seow Wei of ''[[Tatler Asia]]''.

In 2026, she debuted with the movie ''Dream Stall'', about pursuing unorthodox careers in Singapore, which she wrote, directed, and acted in. It was released when Singaporeans were sceptical about locally produced films, and in response, she offered refunds to moviegoers who felt that they "wasted their time".
